# pheno-port-adapter

## Purpose
Hexagonal port-adapter framework for the Phenotype fleet. Provides trait-based ports, swappable adapters, and connection lifecycle management for transport protocols (TCP, UDP, HTTP, Unix sockets).

## When to use
- Building a new network client in the fleet
- Need to swap protocols without changing business logic
- Implementing connection pooling + circuit breaking

## When NOT to use
- Pure HTTP API clients (use reqwest directly)
- One-shot scripts (overhead not worth it)

## Quickstart
```rust
use pheno_port_adapter::prelude::*;
async fn connect() -> Result<TcpStream, Error> {
    let adapter = TcpAdapter::new("127.0.0.1:8080");
    adapter.connect().await
}
```

## Env vars
- `PHENO_TCP_TIMEOUT_MS` — connection timeout (default 5000)
- `PHENO_TCP_RETRY_COUNT` — retry on failure (default 3)

## Key types
- `Port<T>` — trait for pluggable endpoints
- `Adapter<T>` — concrete protocol implementation
- `ConnectionPool<T>` — managed connection lifecycle

## Example
See `pheno-port-adapter/examples/tcp_pool.rs`.
